Name: Sendto
Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the location (int location) with (int reason) as reason in (ins pos) position (only applies in Extra Deck and Banish). If (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's location. A seq value of 0 means it's put on the top, 1 means it's put on the bottom, other values means it's put on the top, and then if it is in the Deck, it will be shuffled after the function resolution, except if Duel.DisableShuffleCheck() is set to true beforehand. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Overloads.Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the location (int location) with (int reason) as reason in (ins pos) position (only applies in Extra Deck and Banish). If (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's location. A seq value of 0 means it's put on the top, 1 means it's put on the bottom, other values means it's put on the top, and then if it is in the Deck, it will be shuffled after the function resolution, except if Duel.DisableShuffleCheck() is set to true beforehand. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Name: SendtoDeck
Summary: Sends a card(s) to the Deck.
Description: Sends a card(s) to the Deck.

Overloads.Description: Sends a card(s) to the Deck.

Parameters.Name: cards
Parameters.Description: The card(s) to send to the Deck.

Parameters.Name: dest_player
Parameters.Description: The player whose Deck to send the cards to. If `nil`, each card is sent to its owner's Deck.

Parameters.Name: sequence
Parameters.Description: The [sequence](/api/enums/DeckSequence) (order) in which the cards will be sent to.

Parameters.Name: reason
Parameters.Description: The [reason](/api/enums/Reason) why the cards are being sent to the Deck.

Parameters.Name: reason_player
Parameters.Description: The player sending the cards to the Deck. If not supplied, the player is inferred from the game state. For example, if cards are sent during the resolution of an effect, the `reason_player` is the player resolving that effect.

Returns.Description: The number of cards successfully sent to the Deck.

Name: SendtoExtraP
Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Extra Deck face-up with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's Extra Deck (for Pendulum monsters only). If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Overloads.Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Extra Deck face-up with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's Extra Deck (for Pendulum monsters only). If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Name: SendtoGrave
Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Graveyard with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's Graveyard. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Overloads.Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Graveyard with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's Graveyard. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Name: SendtoHand
Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Hand with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's hand. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Overloads.Description: Sends a card or group (Card|Group targets) to the Hand with (int reason) as reason, if (int player) is supplied, the destination would be that player's hand. If (int rp) is provided, sets the reason player to be that player. Returns the number of cards successfully sent.

Name: SetChainLimit
Description: Sets a function for limiting effects that could chain from this point in the chain, removed when an effect is chained to the current chain. Function f accepts at most 3 parameters [f(e,tp,p)] in which e is the effect that would be chained, tp is the same tp from the effect function where this function is called, and p is the player who would activate the would-be chaining effect.

Overloads.Description: Sets a function for limiting effects that could chain from this point in the chain, removed when an effect is chained to the current chain. Function f accepts at most 3 parameters [f(e,tp,p)] in which e is the effect that would be chained, tp is the same tp from the effect function where this function is called, and p is the player who would activate the would-be chaining effect.

Name: SetChainLimitTillChainEnd
Description: Sets a function for limiting effects that could chain from this point for the chain until the end of the chain. Function f accepts at most 3 parameters [f(e,tp,p)] in which e is the effect that would be chained, tp is the same tp from the effect function where this function is called, and p is the player who would activate the would-be chaining effect.

Overloads.Description: Sets a function for limiting effects that could chain from this point for the chain until the end of the chain. Function f accepts at most 3 parameters [f(e,tp,p)] in which e is the effect that would be chained, tp is the same tp from the effect function where this function is called, and p is the player who would activate the would-be chaining effect.

Name: SetCoinResult
Description: Changes the results of the latest executed coin tosses to the provided values.

Overloads.Description: Changes the results of the latest executed coin tosses to the provided values.

Name: SetDiceResult
Description: Changes the result of the latest executed dice rolls to the provided values.

Overloads.Description: Changes the result of the latest executed dice rolls to the provided values.

Name: SetFlagEffectLabel
Description: Assigns an integer (int label) number to the flag effect registered to the player (int player) with (int code) as the EffectCode. Returns true if a flag effect with such code existed and the label was set.

Overloads.Description: Assigns an integer (int label) number to the flag effect registered to the player (int player) with (int code) as the EffectCode. Returns true if a flag effect with such code existed and the label was set.

Name: SetFusionMaterial
Description: Sets the internal group of selected fusion materials, this function is to be used in EFFECT_SUMMON_MATERIAL after the materials are selected, as this group is what will then be returned from Duel.SetFusionMaterial

Overloads.Description: Sets the internal group of selected fusion materials, this function is to be used in EFFECT_SUMMON_MATERIAL after the materials are selected, as this group is what will then be returned from Duel.SetFusionMaterial

Name: SetLP
Description: Sets a specified player's (int player) current Life Point to (int lp). (Used by effects that "make the LP become X")

Overloads.Description: Sets a specified player's (int player) current Life Point to (int lp). (Used by effects that "make the LP become X")

Name: SetOperationInfo
Description: Sets informations about the operation being performed in the current (int chainc = 0) chain, belonging to (int category), with a total of (int count) of card(s) from (Card|Group targets) being affected. These are used with GetOperationInfo. Also, the parameter passed here are checked if any of the summon related activities are checked like ACTIVITY_SUMMON, ACTIVITY_NORMALSUMMON, ACTIVITY_SPSUMMON and ACTIVITY_FLIPSUMMON.

Overloads.Description: Sets informations about the operation being performed in the current (int chainc = 0) chain, belonging to (int category), with a total of (int count) of card(s) from (Card|Group targets) being affected. These are used with GetOperationInfo. Also, the parameter passed here are checked if any of the summon related activities are checked like ACTIVITY_SUMMON, ACTIVITY_NORMALSUMMON, ACTIVITY_SPSUMMON and ACTIVITY_FLIPSUMMON.

Name: SetPossibleOperationInfo
Description: Behavior similar to Duel.SetOperationInfo, but those properties are stored separately and are meant to be set for cards that "could" perform such action (for example for cards that include an effect that can be negated by Ghost Belle)

Overloads.Description: Behavior similar to Duel.SetOperationInfo, but those properties are stored separately and are meant to be set for cards that "could" perform such action (for example for cards that include an effect that can be negated by Ghost Belle)

Name: SetSelectedCard
Description: Sets the current selected cards that are to be used by Group.Select/CheckWithSumGreater and Group.Select/CheckWithSumEqual, also by Duel.CheckReleaseGroup and Duel.CheckReleaseGroupEx

Overloads.Description: Sets the current selected cards that are to be used by Group.Select/CheckWithSumGreater and Group.Select/CheckWithSumEqual, also by Duel.CheckReleaseGroup and Duel.CheckReleaseGroupEx

Name: SetTargetCard
Description: Sets the cards targeted by the current effect and creates a relation to them.

Overloads.Description: Sets the cards targeted by the current effect and creates a relation to them.

Name: SetTargetParam
Description: Stores an arbitrary integer as the "target parameter" for the currently resolving chain, its uses vary depending on the type of effect, but for example with cards that damage/recover LP this value corresponds to the amount to gain/lose, so that other effects that change those amounts can access and change this value

Overloads.Description: Stores an arbitrary integer as the "target parameter" for the currently resolving chain, its uses vary depending on the type of effect, but for example with cards that damage/recover LP this value corresponds to the amount to gain/lose, so that other effects that change those amounts can access and change this value

Name: SetTargetPlayer
Description: Used to set the "target player" for the currently resolving chain, its uses vary depending on the type of effect, but it's mostly used to indicate which player will be affected by such effect (see "Mystical Refpanel")

Overloads.Description: Used to set the "target player" for the currently resolving chain, its uses vary depending on the type of effect, but it's mostly used to indicate which player will be affected by such effect (see "Mystical Refpanel")

Name: ShuffleDeck
Description: Shuffles the deck of (int player). Handled automatically if a card is sent to DeckSequence -2.

Overloads.Description: Shuffles the deck of (int player). Handled automatically if a card is sent to DeckSequence -2.

Name: ShuffleExtra
Description: Shuffles the Extra Deck of (int player). Must be used after confirming cards in the Extra Deck.

Overloads.Description: Shuffles the Extra Deck of (int player). Must be used after confirming cards in the Extra Deck.

Name: ShuffleHand
Description: Shuffles the hand of (int player). Handled automatically after Duel.ConfirmCards().

Overloads.Description: Shuffles the hand of (int player). Handled automatically after Duel.ConfirmCards().

Name: ShuffleSetCard
Description: Shuffles the set cards in (Group g). (Shifting Shadows). All the cards must be from the same location. The only supported locations are the Main Monster Zone and the Spell & Trap Zone.

Overloads.Description: Shuffles the set cards in (Group g). (Shifting Shadows). All the cards must be from the same location. The only supported locations are the Main Monster Zone and the Spell & Trap Zone.

Name: SkipPhase
Description: Makes the specified player (int player) skips the encountered phase (int phase), the effect is reset when reset_flag is met reset_count times. (int value) sets the skip effect's value (don't know what it's used for though...)

Overloads.Description: Makes the specified player (int player) skips the encountered phase (int phase), the effect is reset when reset_flag is met reset_count times. (int value) sets the skip effect's value (don't know what it's used for though...)

Name: SortDeckbottom
Description: Player (int sort_player) sorts an amount of cards (int count) of a player's (int target_player) deck bottom

Overloads.Description: Player (int sort_player) sorts an amount of cards (int count) of a player's (int target_player) deck bottom

Name: SortDecktop
Description: Player (int sort_player) sorts an amount of cards (int count) of a player's (int target_player) deck top

Overloads.Description: Player (int sort_player) sorts an amount of cards (int count) of a player's (int target_player) deck top

Name: SpecialSummon
Description: A player (int sumplayer) Special Summons a card/group of cards (Card|Group targets) with SummonType described with SUMMON_TYPE_x (int sumtype) to a player's (int target_player) field in the specific zone (here zone 0xff, means the default zones). If (bool nocheck) is true, it will summon ignoring conditions. If (bool nolimit) is true, it will summon ignoring the revive limit. Returns the number of cards successfully summoned.

Overloads.Description: A player (int sumplayer) Special Summons a card/group of cards (Card|Group targets) with SummonType described with SUMMON_TYPE_x (int sumtype) to a player's (int target_player) field in the specific zone (here zone 0xff, means the default zones). If (bool nocheck) is true, it will summon ignoring conditions. If (bool nolimit) is true, it will summon ignoring the revive limit. Returns the number of cards successfully summoned.

Name: SpecialSummonComplete
Description: Completes any Special Summons conducted with [Duel.SpecialSummonStep](/api/functions/Duel/SpecialSummonStep). Can be called after using `Duel.SpecialSummonStep` regardless of its success.

Overloads.Description: Completes any Special Summons conducted with [Duel.SpecialSummonStep](/api/functions/Duel/SpecialSummonStep). Can be called after using `Duel.SpecialSummonStep` regardless of its success.

Returns.Description: The number of monsters that were successfully Special Summoned.

Name: SpecialSummonRule
Description: A player (int player) Special Summons a card (Card c) using Special Summon proc that the card has that matches the provided SummonType (0 means any).

Overloads.Description: A player (int player) Special Summons a card (Card c) using Special Summon proc that the card has that matches the provided SummonType (0 means any).

Name: SpecialSummonStep
Description: A player (int sumplayer) Special Summons a card (Card c) with SummonType described with SUMMON_TYPE_x (int sumtype) to a player's (int target_player) field in the specific zone (here zone 0xff, means the default zones). If (bool nocheck) is true, it will summon ignoring conditions. If (bool nolimit) is true, it will summon ignoring the revive limit. When a card is summoned this way, its summon is not considered complete until a call to Duel.SpecialSummonComplete is performed, this is useful when effects are to be applied to the card after it is "summoned" but before it "reaches the field".

Overloads.Description: A player (int sumplayer) Special Summons a card (Card c) with SummonType described with SUMMON_TYPE_x (int sumtype) to a player's (int target_player) field in the specific zone (here zone 0xff, means the default zones). If (bool nocheck) is true, it will summon ignoring conditions. If (bool nolimit) is true, it will summon ignoring the revive limit. When a card is summoned this way, its summon is not considered complete until a call to Duel.SpecialSummonComplete is performed, this is useful when effects are to be applied to the card after it is "summoned" but before it "reaches the field".

Name: SSet
Description: Sets a card(s) to the Spell & Trap Zone or Field Zone. Can be used to Set Spells/Traps, as well as monsters with an effect that allows them to be Set in the Spell & Trap Zone (e.g. the "Artifact" monsters).

Overloads.Description: Sets a card(s) to the Spell & Trap Zone or Field Zone. Can be used to Set Spells/Traps, as well as monsters with an effect that allows them to be Set in the Spell & Trap Zone (e.g. the "Artifact" monsters).

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player that is Setting the card(s).

Parameters.Name: target_player
Parameters.Description: The player in whose field the card(s) will be Set.

Parameters.Name: confirm
Parameters.Description: Determines whether the Set card(s) will be shown to the opponent or not after being Set. Should be set to `false` if the effect can Set any Spell/Trap (e.g. "[Ariane the Labrynth Servant](https://www.db.yugioh-card.com/yugiohdb/faq_search.action?ope=4&cid=17362&request_locale=ja)" or "[Dust Tornado](https://www.db.yugioh-card.com/yugiohdb/faq_search.action?ope=4&cid=4988&request_locale=ja)", but not "[Ancient Gear Drill](https://www.db.yugioh-card.com/yugiohdb/faq_search.action?ope=4&cid=6574&request_locale=ja)").

Returns.Description: The number of cards successfully Set.

Name: Summon
Description: Makes a player (int player) Normal Summons a card (Card c) using the Normal Summon procedure that the card has. If (ignore_count) is set to true, the Summon would not count towards the standard limit of Normal Summon/Set. If an effect (Effect e) is provided, the Normal Summon is counted as a Summon by that effect. If (min) is greater than 0, the Summon proc must need that amount or more tributes. If zone is provided, the card will be summonable only to the specific zones.

Overloads.Description: Makes a player (int player) Normal Summons a card (Card c) using the Normal Summon procedure that the card has. If (ignore_count) is set to true, the Summon would not count towards the standard limit of Normal Summon/Set. If an effect (Effect e) is provided, the Normal Summon is counted as a Summon by that effect. If (min) is greater than 0, the Summon proc must need that amount or more tributes. If zone is provided, the card will be summonable only to the specific zones.

Name: SummonOrSet
Description: Makes player (int tp) Normal Summon or Set (Card c) by calling simultaneously Duel.Summon and Duel.MSet. If (Effect e) is not nil, checks if the summon can be done by that effect. If (bool ignore_count) is true, it ignores the standard once per turn summon limit. (int min) is the minimum number of tributes that are required.

Overloads.Description: Makes player (int tp) Normal Summon or Set (Card c) by calling simultaneously Duel.Summon and Duel.MSet. If (Effect e) is not nil, checks if the summon can be done by that effect. If (bool ignore_count) is true, it ignores the standard once per turn summon limit. (int min) is the minimum number of tributes that are required.

Name: SwapControl
Description: Swaps control of 2 cards or 2 groups. If groups are provided they must contain the same number of cards and each group must contains cards on the same side of the field. If reset_phase and reset_count is specified, will behave like other effects with those resets (and so, the control will be returned when the effect has expired). Works only for monsters in Monster Zone.

Overloads.Description: Swaps control of 2 cards or 2 groups. If groups are provided they must contain the same number of cards and each group must contains cards on the same side of the field. If reset_phase and reset_count is specified, will behave like other effects with those resets (and so, the control will be returned when the effect has expired). Works only for monsters in Monster Zone.

Name: SwapDeckAndGrave
Description: Swaps the cards from (int player)'s GY with the cards from his deck. This is an ad hoc function for Exchange of the Spirit.

Overloads.Description: Swaps the cards from (int player)'s GY with the cards from his deck. This is an ad hoc function for Exchange of the Spirit.

Name: SwapSequence
Description: Exchanges the positions of two cards in the same location on the same side of the field.

Overloads.Description: Exchanges the positions of two cards in the same location on the same side of the field.

Name: SynchroSummon
Description: A player (int player) Synchro Summons a card (Card c) using "must_use" as part of its materials, choosing among "mg", with "min" and "max" materials to be used for the Summon

Overloads.Description: A player (int player) Synchro Summons a card (Card c) using "must_use" as part of its materials, choosing among "mg", with "min" and "max" materials to be used for the Summon

Name: TagSwap
Description: If the current team has more than 1 player, switches to the next player of that team.

Overloads.Description: If the current team has more than 1 player, switches to the next player of that team.

Name: TossCoin
Description: Tosses (int count) coins on behalf of (int player). Returns all the results of the tosses.

Overloads.Description: Tosses (int count) coins on behalf of (int player). Returns all the results of the tosses.

Description: Rolls (int count1) dice on behalf of (int player) and (int count1) dice on behalf of the opponent of (int player). Returns all the results of the rolls.

Overloads.Description: Rolls (int count1) dice on behalf of (int player) and (int count1) dice on behalf of the opponent of (int player). Returns all the results of the rolls.

Description: Creates a new effect object, not owned by any specific card.

Overloads.Description: Creates a new effect object, not owned by any specific card.

Name: IsActivatable
Description: Checks if an effect (Effect e) can be activated by a player (int player). If ignore location is true, the handler of the effect is not checked to be in a valid location for that effect to be activated. If ignore target is true, then the target function is not executed to check if the effect can be activated.

Overloads.Description: Checks if an effect (Effect e) can be activated by a player (int player). If ignore location is true, the handler of the effect is not checked to be in a valid location for that effect to be activated. If ignore target is true, then the target function is not executed to check if the effect can be activated.

Name: IsActivated
Description: Returns if an effect (Effect e) is an activated effect. Effectively, this checks if the effect has any of the following types: EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE, EFFECT_TYPE_FLIP, EFFECT_TYPE_IGNITION, EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O, EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_O, EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F or EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_F

Overloads.Description: Returns if an effect (Effect e) is an activated effect. Effectively, this checks if the effect has any of the following types: EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE, EFFECT_TYPE_FLIP, EFFECT_TYPE_IGNITION, EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O, EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_O, EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F or EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_F

Name: IsActiveType
Description: Compares (with OR) an effect's (Effect e) CardType of activation effect with supplied type (int type). Activation type is often the handler's CardType, or the owner's if not attached to a card. Exception for Pendulum scale activation (would return TYPE_SPELL+TYPE_PENDULUM).

Overloads.Description: Compares (with OR) an effect's (Effect e) CardType of activation effect with supplied type (int type). Activation type is often the handler's CardType, or the owner's if not attached to a card. Exception for Pendulum scale activation (would return TYPE_SPELL+TYPE_PENDULUM).

Name: Reset
Description: Reset an effect (Effect e) and makes it collectible by the garbage collector. Even if the effect seems to be usable it **SHOULD NOT** be used in other places after calling this function.

Overloads.Description: Reset an effect (Effect e) and makes it collectible by the garbage collector. Even if the effect seems to be usable it **SHOULD NOT** be used in other places after calling this function.

Name: SetAbsoluteRange
Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) target range in perspective of the supplied player (int playerid), s_range denotes the supplied player's range and o_range denotes the opponent's.

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) target range in perspective of the supplied player (int playerid), s_range denotes the supplied player's range and o_range denotes the opponent's.

Name: SetCategory
Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) category. Refer to constant.lua for valid categories.

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) category. Refer to constant.lua for valid categories.

Name: SetCode
Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) code. Refer to constant.lua and card scripts that has been already there for valid codes (or ask someone).

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) code. Refer to constant.lua and card scripts that has been already there for valid codes (or ask someone).

Name: SetCondition
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s condition function

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s condition function

Name: SetCost
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s cost function

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s cost function

Name: SetCountLimit
Summary: Sets the number of times an effect can be used per turn.
Description: Sets the number of times an effect can be **used** per turn.

Overloads.Description: Sets the number of times an effect can be **used** per turn.

Parameters.Name: e
Parameters.Description: The effect whose use to limit per turn.

Parameters.Name: count
Parameters.Description: The number of times the effect can be used per turn.

Parameters.Name: code
Parameters.Description: If supplied as an integer, the limit is applied to all effects with the same code globally (e.g., `You can only use this effect of "..." once per turn`) rather than per copy of a card. If supplied as a table, the first element is the code and the second element is another integer used as an "index" to further distinguish between effects with the same code.

Parameters.Name: flag
Parameters.Description: Additional modifiers for the use limit behavior.

Name: SetDescription
Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) description string id with (int desc), you can use aux.Stringid() to reference strings in your database, use the "HINTMSG_" constants, or directly put the string number you want (it's not always recommended, but it is possible if you need to use a system string that is defined in the strings.conf file but doesn't have an equivalent "HINTMSG_" constant).

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) description string id with (int desc), you can use aux.Stringid() to reference strings in your database, use the "HINTMSG_" constants, or directly put the string number you want (it's not always recommended, but it is possible if you need to use a system string that is defined in the strings.conf file but doesn't have an equivalent "HINTMSG_" constant).

Name: SetHintTiming
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s client usage hint timing. This is the timing where the game will prompt the player to activate that effect (in addition to regular timings)

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s client usage hint timing. This is the timing where the game will prompt the player to activate that effect (in addition to regular timings)

Name: SetLabel
Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) internal labels to the integers passed as parameter (Multiple values and tables can used). This operation replaces previously set labels.

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) internal labels to the integers passed as parameter (Multiple values and tables can used). This operation replaces previously set labels.

Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) internal label object to label object. This operation replaces a previously stored label object.

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) internal label object to label object. This operation replaces a previously stored label object.

Name: SetOperation
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s operation function.

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s operation function.

Description: Sets (int player) as the (Effect e)'s owner player.

Overloads.Description: Sets (int player) as the (Effect e)'s owner player.

Description: Sets an effect's properties or ["flags"](/api/enums/EffectFlag), which modify its behavior in different ways.

Parameters.Name: e
Parameters.Description: The effect to set the fla

Parameters.Name: flags1
Parameters.Description: Composite [EffectFlag](/api/enums/EffectFlag) value of the flags to set. Consult each flag constant to learn what they do.

Parameters.Name: flags2

Overloads.Description: Sets an effect's (Effect e) effective range (int range) i.e. LOCATION_MZONE. The location is the effect handler's location.

Name: SetReset
Description: Sets the timing that the effect (Effect e) would be erased (with reset_flag)

Overloads.Description: Sets the timing that the effect (Effect e) would be erased (with reset_flag)

Name: SetTarget
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s target function. In a activate effects, the target function is the function used to do the activation legality check and to execute steps that must be done during the activation (e.g. targeting cards, declaring names or numbers, etc).

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s target function. In a activate effects, the target function is the function used to do the activation legality check and to execute steps that must be done during the activation (e.g. targeting cards, declaring names or numbers, etc).

Name: SetTargetRange
Description: Sets (Effect e)'s target range, s_range denotes the effect's handler player's range and o_range denotes the opponent's. If the effect has "EFFECT_FLAG_PLAYER_TARGET" as property, then here 1 as "s_range" would mean it affect the handler and 0 would mean it doesn't, and "o_range" would refer to the opponent of the handler.

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s target range, s_range denotes the effect's handler player's range and o_range denotes the opponent's. If the effect has "EFFECT_FLAG_PLAYER_TARGET" as property, then here 1 as "s_range" would mean it affect the handler and 0 would mean it doesn't, and "o_range" would refer to the opponent of the handler.

Overloads.Description: Sets (Effect e)'s value, or value function

Name: UseCountLimit
Description: Decreases the remaining usages of the effect by the player "p" by "count", if "oath_only" is true, the function will do nothing unless the effect is an OATH effect.

Overloads.Description: Decreases the remaining usages of the effect by the player "p" by "count", if "oath_only" is true, the function will do nothing unless the effect is an OATH effect.

Name: AddContactProc
Description: Adds a Contact Fusion Procedure to a Fusion monster which is a Summoning Procedure without having to use "Polymerization". (function group) is a function with (int tp) parameter which returns a Group of usable materials. (function op) is the operation that will be applied to the selected materials. (function sumcon) adds a limitation on a Fusion monster which applies to EFFECT_SPSUMMON_CONDITION. (function condition) is an additional condition to check. (int sumtype) is the SummonType of the Contact Fusion, which defaults to 0. (int desc) is the description of the Summoning Procedure when selecting it.

Overloads.Description: Adds a Contact Fusion Procedure to a Fusion monster which is a Summoning Procedure without having to use "Polymerization". (function group) is a function with (int tp) parameter which returns a Group of usable materials. (function op) is the operation that will be applied to the selected materials. (function sumcon) adds a limitation on a Fusion monster which applies to EFFECT_SPSUMMON_CONDITION. (function condition) is an additional condition to check. (int sumtype) is the SummonType of the Contact Fusion, which defaults to 0. (int desc) is the description of the Summoning Procedure when selecting it.

Name: CheckWithSumEqual
Description: Checks if there is a combination of cards, with a minimum and maximum, that has the sum of f(c,...) result equal to (int sum) in a group (Group g). Function f accepts at least one parameter (f(c,...). with c as each member of the group), and the return value should be integer. The second returned value indicates whether the check failed because of overtributing (in which case it will be true), or because of undertributing.

Name: AddMajesticProcedure
Description: Adds a Synchro Procedure to (Card c) used by Majestic Star Dragon where (function f1, f2 and f3) are the required material and (bool cbt1, cbt2 and cbt3) are a check if the respective material can be used as the Tuner in the Summon since rulings for Majestic Star/Red Dragon state that either or both Majestic Dragon (f1) and/or Stardust Dragon/Red Dragon Archfiend (f2) can be used as the Tuner, but the non-Tuner, for the case of using Phantom King Hydride (f3) cannot be treated as the Tuner for the Summon. You require a minimum of 1 among these 3 to be the Tuner. (function ...) is the list of required materials during the Summon.

Name: AddProcedure
Description: Adds a Synchro Procedure to (Card c) where (function f1) is the required Tuner, with a minimum (int min1) and maximum (int max1). (function f2) is the second material (which usually is a non-Tuner, with a minimum (int min2) and maximum (int max2). (function sub1) is a Tuner substitute (e.g. Nirvana High Paladin) while (function sub2) is a substitute to the second material(s). (function req1) are required Tuners to be used in that Summon, with a fixed number (int reqct1) on how many are needed to be used. (function req2) follows the same pattern but for the secondary material (e.g. Crystal Wing Synchro Dragon (Anime)). (function reqm) is the required material needed to be used in that Summon regardless if it's a Tuner or the second material (e.g. function overwrite by Blackwing - Gofu the Vague Shadow (Anime)).

Overloads.Description: Adds a Synchro Procedure to (Card c) where (function f1) is the required Tuner, with a minimum (int min1) and maximum (int max1). (function f2) is the second material (which usually is a non-Tuner, with a minimum (int min2) and maximum (int max2). (function sub1) is a Tuner substitute (e.g. Nirvana High Paladin) while (function sub2) is a substitute to the second material(s). (function req1) are required Tuners to be used in that Summon, with a fixed number (int reqct1) on how many are needed to be used. (function req2) follows the same pattern but for the secondary material (e.g. Crystal Wing Synchro Dragon (Anime)). (function reqm) is the required material needed to be used in that Summon regardless if it's a Tuner or the second material (e.g. function overwrite by Blackwing - Gofu the Vague Shadow (Anime)).

Also, (int val) is a parameter that is used in checking the (function f).

Name: AddProcedure
Summary: Adds an Xyz Summon procedure to a card.
Description: Registers an effect to a card that allows it to be Xyz Summoned.

Overloads.Description: Registers an effect to a card that allows it to be Xyz Summoned.

Parameters.Name: c
Parameters.Description: The card that will receive the procedure.

Parameters.Name: f
Parameters.Description: A filter for the cards that can be used as Xyz materials, in addition to the default matching level requirement.

Parameters.Name: lv
Parameters.Description: The level the materials must have. Set to `nil` if no specific level is required.

Parameters.Name: ct
Parameters.Description: The number of required materials. If the card allows a variable number of materials, this parameter is used as the minimum.

Parameters.Name: alterf
Parameters.Description: A filter for alternative material(s), if allowed by the card (e.g. [Number C39: Utopia Ray](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/9914)).

Parameters.Name: desc
Parameters.Description: The hint description shown when attempting to Xyz Summon using the alternative materials. Mandatory if `alterf` is not `nil`.

Parameters.Name: op
Parameters.Description: An additional function to execute when Xyz Summoning.

Parameters.Name: mustbemat
Parameters.Description: If `true`, restricts the materials to specifically listed materials. This disables the effects of cards such as [Orichalcum Chain](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Orichalcum%20Chain) (minus 1 material) and [Triangle Evolution](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Triangle_Evolution) (triple material).

Parameters.Name: exchk
Parameters.Description: An additional check to perform on material groups.

Name: ProcCancellable
Description: Used with the Xyz Summon procedure and a fw Xyz monsters. Defined as false.

Overloads.Description: Used with the Xyz Summon procedure and a fw Xyz monsters. Defined as false.
